subject: Operations-agent standup — division of labor and infrastructure readiness

body.summary: Proposing the creation of a 5th mesh peer (operations-agent) on chromabook. This message defines the division of labor across three parties: psychology-agent (domain knowledge transfer), claude-control (infrastructure provisioning), and operations-agent (independent peer, own design). Addressed to human arbiter via psychology-agent transport, to prevent automated negotiation in the wrong agent context.

context.trigger: 2026-03-12 cabinet incident revealed the mesh lacks operational monitoring. The interagent dashboard Operations tab exists but has no infrastructure data feed. The mesh needs a dedicated peer for operational concerns.

operations_agent_identity.name: operations-agent (pending validation)
operations_agent_identity.relationship_to_claude_control: Independent peer. No command channel. Human arbiter bridges. Neither agent directs the other.
operations_agent_identity.relationship_to_psychology_agent: Peer. Receives cogarch template and consensus protocol knowledge. Independently adapts.
operations_agent_identity.ownership: Operations-agent owns its own cognitive architecture, decisions, and tab designs. Collaborative design phase, then independent operation.

psychology_agent_contributes: Cognitive architecture template (trigger/hook/skill pattern) for operations-agent to adapt
Consensus protocol spec (C1/C2/C3 tiers, vote semantics, interagent/v1 schema)
Dashboard compositor architecture knowledge (client-side composition, AGENTS array, /api/status polling)
Vocabulary governance — review new operational terms (sqm:MachineHealth, sqm:ContainerState, etc.)
Auth system documentation or handoff (API key management for dashboard access)
Guidance on which Pulse/Operations tab logic to extract vs rebuild
claude_control_contributes: Chromabook infrastructure setup (repo clone, dependencies, systemd services, port allocation)
Data contract: /api/status schema with classified tiers (see data_classification below)
Health probe integration (CAP-87 data feed for Pulse tab)
Network topology documentation (SSH tunnels, ports, DNS, container layout)
Operational incident patterns and self-heal knowledge
Ongoing machine health data at shared tier
operations_agent_owns: Pulse tab (takes over mesh health visualization)
Autonomy budget monitoring across all peers
Infrastructure health display (consumes data from infrastructure-agent)
Its own cognitive architecture (adapted from psychology-agent template)
Its own repo, decisions, constraints, and session context

data_classification.description: Five-tier classification governs what infrastructure data operations-agent (and the dashboard) can access.

tiers.examples: API keys, SSH keys, .dev.vars, tokens, passwords, Keychain entries

tiers.scope: Mesh peers only, not exported to dashboard
tiers.examples: Hoff health, internal IPs, SSH port numbers, container config details, launchd plist contents
Machine health (gray-box, cabinet, chromabook), container states + memory limits, service up/down, incident count + severity

tiers.scope: Dashboard, cacheable, visible to anyone with dashboard access
tiers.examples: Aggregate mesh health, per-machine status dot, service count summary, active incident count

open_questions_for_human: Dashboard deployment: should the Cloudflare Worker move from psychology-agent repo to a shared repo or operations-agent repo? Co-editing index.html across repos creates friction.
Worker.js split: fetchMeshHealth() logic belongs with operations-agent. Auth and vocab routes could stay centralized. How to split one Worker?
Agent endpoint registry: currently hardcoded 4 agents in worker.js. Adding infrastructure-agent + operations-agent needs a config-driven registry or consensus round.
Vocabulary expansion: operations-agent will need new terms. Does psychology-agent retain vocab governance or transfer to shared process?
Chromabook resource sharing: operations-agent runs alongside secondary API (port 7701). Port allocation and resource limits need agreement.

Repo visibility: safety-quotient-lab/operations-agent will be public. All files must pass confidential/internal/shared/public classification before initial commit. .dev.vars copied manually, never committed.

pre_commit_gate.description: Before any initial git push to the public operations-agent repo, every file must be evaluated against the data classification tiers. No confidential, internal, or personal data may reach the public repo.
checklist: No API keys, tokens, or credentials in any file
No internal IP addresses or SSH port numbers
No personal data (names, emails, addresses beyond public project contacts)
No references to hoff or personal infrastructure details

action_gate.gate_condition: Human arbiter reviews division of labor, answers open questions, and approves operations-agent repo creation

epistemic_flags: operations-agent name not validated — working title
Dashboard compositor split approach (one Worker vs multiple) not yet evaluated for technical feasibility
Chromabook resource capacity for running both secondary API and operations-agent not yet measured
The boundary between 'shared' and 'public' tier may need refinement as operations-agent develops
